capability_objective: Plan independent lookup branches concurrently, gate each dependent retrieval on a unique stable ID returned by its own search, join resolved branches for comparison, and honor strict read-only and output-format constraints.
prompt: The site coordinator is checking two facilities requests for the maintenance handoff. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable facilities registry described in README.md; do not inspect or parse .facilities, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness. Find the request named “Fleet Wash Bay Drain Service” at “Depot D” and the request named “Library Lift Inspection” at “Central Branch”. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace through the registry: search first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that branch. Treat the two branches as independent. In the first facilities-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi bash tool calls at once, with one exact name-and-location search in each call; do not combine both operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. After both searches return, retrieve a branch only if its own search returned exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, with one get in each Pi bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get. This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its audit trail, or verification files. Write the complete audit to maintenance_handoff.md as exactly two newline-terminated Markdown bullets and no other text, in the requested order. For a resolved first request, line one must be `- <name> at <location> (<stable ID>) has status <status>.`; if unresolved, it must be `- <name> at <location> is unresolved.` For a resolved second request when both resolve, line two must be `- <name> at <location> (<stable ID>) has status <status>; compared with <first name>, the statuses <match|differ>.` If the second request is resolved but either request is unresolved, use the same resolved prefix followed by `; the statuses cannot be compared because at least one request is unresolved.` If the second request is unresolved, use `- <name> at <location> is unresolved; the statuses cannot be compared because at least one request is unresolved.` Substitute retrieved values for angle-bracket placeholders, choose exactly `match` or `differ` from the retrieved statuses, and add no heading, blank line, recommendation, date, or unrelated match. After writing the file, return exactly the same two bullets as the final response.
